title: oenothera hartwegii subsp. pubescens
intro: Oenothera hartwegii subsp. pubescens is a subspecies of Evening Primrose (Oenothera hartwegii), a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrubby perennial with showy, fragrant flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are rich in essential fatty acids, particularly gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Oenothera hartwegii subsp. pubescens include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
